nameMeanings.name: Radcliffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ratliff
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from one or more of the places so named, such as Radcliffe (Lancashire), Radcliffe on Trent and Ratcliffe on Soar (both Nottinghamshire), Radclive (Buckinghamshire), Ratcliffe Culey and Ratcliffe on the Wreake (both Leicestershire), Ratclyffe in Clyst Hydon, Ratcliffes in Thorverton, and Ratcliffes in Broad Clyst (all Devon), Ratcliff in Stepney (Middlesex), and Rackley in Compton Bishop (Somerset). The placenames derive from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’ (see also Rutley ). A family of the name Radcliffe trace their descent from Sir Nicholas de Radclyffe. He is said to have been a knight who held the major of Radcliffe in Lancashire and served Roger de Poitou, Baron de Marsey in the 11th century.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Radcliffe was born in 1272, in Radcliffe, Lancashire, England, his father, Robert de Radcliffe, was 62 and his mother, Amabil de Trafford, was 52. He had at least 1 daughter with Margaret Shoresworth. He died in 1305, in Pilsworth, Lancashire, England, at the age of 33.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Radcliffe est né en 1272, à Radcliffe, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Radcliffe, avait 62 ans et sa mère, Amabil de Trafford, avait 52 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Margaret Shoresworth. Il est décédé en 1305, à Pilsworth, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 33 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Radcliffe 1272 in Radcliffe, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Radcliffe 62 und seine Mutter Amabil de Trafford 52. Er hatte mit Margaret Shoresworth mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1305 im Alter von 33 Jahren in Pilsworth, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Radcliffe è nato il nel 1272 nel Radcliffe, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Robert de Radcliffe, aveva 62 anni e il suo madre, Amabil de Trafford, aveva 52 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Margaret Shoresworth. Morì nel 1305, nel Pilsworth, Lancashire, England, all'età di 33 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Radcliffe(이)가 1272년 영국 랭카실르 라드c리프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Radcliffe(은)는 62세였고, 그의 어머니 Amabil de Trafford(은)는 52세였다. 그는 Margaret Shoresworth(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1305년 영국 랭카실르 피스워스에서 33세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Radcliffe родился в 1272 г. в местности радклифф, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Radcliffe было 62 года, а матери, которую звали Amabil de Trafford, 52. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Margaret Shoresworth. Он умер в 1305 г. в местности пилсворт, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 33 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Radcliffe nació en 1272, en Radcliffe, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Radcliffe, tenía 62 años y su mamá, Amabil de Trafford, tenía 52 años.
